pi_items.source: CSV data extracted from setup_task.sh contains 10 rows of item data
CSV data shows 4 items with Return Date values: Educated Book (2024-12-05), Camping Tent (2024-12-18), Hedge Trimmer (2024-12-08), Instant Pot (2024-12-22)
CSV data shows 6 items with blank Return Date: Circular Saw, Pressure Washer, Pasta Maker, Board Game Wingspan, Kayak Paddle, Extension Ladder
Sum of unreturned item values: Circular Saw ($120) + Pressure Washer ($200) + Pasta Maker ($80) + Board Game Wingspan ($60) + Kayak Paddle ($65) + Extension Ladder ($175) = $700

pi_items.metadata_value: Items exceeding 30 days on loan should be highlighted
pi_items.verified_value: All 6 unreturned items are over 30 days old (range 462-600 days as of 2026-03-07)
pi_items.source: Date calculation: Lent dates range from 2024-07-15 to 2024-11-30, compared to reference date 2026-03-07. Shortest: Board Game (462 days), Longest: Kayak Paddle (600 days)
task.json init.timeout_sec field confirms 180 second timeout
task.json init.max_steps field confirms 15 step maximum
task.json difficulty field and README confirm medium difficulty
verifier.py line 326 explicitly documents expected_total = 700, which matches actual SUMIF result from unreturned items
Complete read of task.json shows only fields: id, version, env_id, description, difficulty, natural_language, init, hooks, success

privileged_info_summary: The task uses a synthetic lending tracker dataset with 10 items, but contains a significant inconsistency: the setup script claims '5 returned, 5 still out' while the actual CSV data contains 4 returned and 6 unreturned items totaling $700 in unreturned value. As of March 7, 2026, all unreturned items exceed 30 days on loan (range 462-600 days), requiring conditional formatting highlighting. The task is configured with 180-second timeout and 15-step maximum, with medium difficulty. Importantly, the task.json file does not contain a 'metadata' field as expected by the audit framework.
